text: fat is 0.2g. omega 3 is doing serious work here. for a tuber, the cleanest win is a more stable carb profile.
carbs is 26.5g. the fibre support does a lot of the heavy lifting. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.
protein is 1.5g. protein quality is clearly one of the selling points. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.
vitamin a at 0% DV, vitamin c at 7% DV stand out most. that is not enough to move the verdict very far on its own.
potassium at 13% DV, magnesium at 8% DV, iron at 2% DV stand out most. that stays fairly limited overall.

pros first. practical staple carb source because tubers are often judged by how usable they are day to day. whole-food carb package because that matters because it is a cleaner baseline than heavily refined carbs. can be quite filling because a useful practical strength when the preparation stays sensible.

the drawbacks next. still a carb-heavy category because that is the core ceiling tubers have to manage. preparation changes outcomes a lot because what you do to a tuber can make or break it. protein support is weak because tubers still need help from the rest of the meal.
Taro is A tier. Best when you want practical carbs and the rest of the profile is still reasonably clean.
